questions.text: What is the intended lifespan of examples/hello.py? (Author/PR owner: The PR description says 'safe to close/delete once the run is validated' — who is responsible for that cleanup, and by what date or milestone?)

Is the lack of input validation in greet(name) intentional? (Developer: The function performs no validation of empty strings, None, or special characters — is this intentional minimalism for a smoke target, or should input validation be added even to example code?)
Is examples/ the canonical location for smoke-test analysis targets in this project? (Architect/Tech Lead: Is there a CONTRIBUTING guide or README section that establishes this convention?)
Should examples/ be a proper Python package (with __init__.py) to support imports? (Architect/Developer: Currently standalone execution only — is this intentional?)

Should a companion test file (e.g. tests/test_hello.py) be added alongside examples/hello.py? (Developer: No tests were added in this PR — is the module intentionally test-free because it is only a protocol analysis target?)

After the smoke test is validated, who owns closing/deleting this PR and removing examples/hello.py from main? (Author/PR owner: Is there a tracking issue or just a manual action, and on what timeline?)
